Lynn County

Introduction to Lynn County

Arrows in Lynn County

Arrow #16: Tahoka

More Details for Arrow #16

Coordinates: 33.1665615, -101.7974021

Installed: December 16, 2011

Marker Text:

Lynn County's lakes and playas once were favorite campsites for Quanah Parker and nomadic Indians

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #17: O'Donnell

More Details for Arrow #17

Coordinates: 32.9647607, -101.8328095

Installed: December 16, 2011

Dedicated: August 27, 2016

Marker Text:

Comanches traveling E/W on Tahoka Lake Trail camped in the canyons East of present-day O'Donnell

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #24: O'Donnell

More Details for Arrow #24

Coordinates: 32.9796591, -101.8295911

Installed: February 3, 2012

Marker Text:

Comanches ascended the High Plains at points NE and S of O'Donnell using Mooar's Draw and TJF Draw

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #29: Stud Duck Ranch

More Details for Arrow #29

Coordinates: 33.1670859, -101.9548094

Installed: February 6, 2012

Marker Text:

None

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #82: Tahoka Lake Pasture

More Details for Arrow #82

Coordinates: 33.2588336, -101.7475026

Installed: March 18, 2016

Dedicated: August 27, 2016

Marker Text:

1939 Quanah's son Baldwin visited and said Comanche elders recalled fresh water at their Tahoka Lake campsite

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #88: New Home

More Details for Arrow #88

Coordinates: 33.3264677, -101.9125109

Installed: None

Marker Text:

None

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Texas Plains Trail Region Map

Click a county to view arrows

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
